articles.title: CrowdStrike and Google take down botnet used by hackers to target software developers in supply chain attacks
articles.description: Cybercriminals used the Glassworm botnet to infect open source software projects with malware, and in turn hack the developers and companies that use that software.

articles.title: AI coding startup Cognition raises $1B at $25B pre-money valuation
articles.description: As Cognition reaches $492 million in annualized revenue run rate, it more than doubled its valuation in eight months, it says.

articles.title: FAA orders SpaceX to investigate Starship V3 booster failure
articles.description: Starship is grounded until SpaceX finds out why the first V3 booster failed during its first test flight.

articles.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close today: Nominate a founder or submit your startup
articles.description: Today is the final day to apply or nominate a startup for Startup Battlefield 200. Once the clock strikes 11:59 p.m. PT, the window closes on your chance to compete for $100,000 in equity-free funding, gain global visibility, connect directly with investors, and launch on the TechCrunch Disrupt stage.

articles.title: ElevenLabs’ new music-generation model can switch genres mid-track
articles.description: ElevenLabs' new model will let users regenerate a section of a song without affecting the rest of the track.

articles.title: Spotify now lets you ‘clip’ moments from your favorite podcast
articles.description: With the new scissors icon, you can clip favorite moments from podcasts and share them with your audience.

articles.title: SOND, a sleep tech startup from Bose’s former head of sleep, exits stealth with $7M
articles.description: SOND, a startup led by Bose’s former head of sleep products, emerged from stealth with $7M in funding for its AI-powered sleep earbuds.

articles.title: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 Early Bird ticket savings end in 3 days
articles.description: There are only 3 days left to save up to $410 on your ticket to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Early Bird pricing ends May 29 at 11:59 p.m. PT, and once the deadline passes, ticket prices increase. If you plan to attend one of the most influential gatherings in tech this year, now is the time to lock in your pass before rates go up again.

articles.title: China is increasingly keeping its best AI talent to itself
articles.description: China's AI boom is producing world-class talent, and Beijing is increasingly reluctant to let them go elsewhere.

articles.title: ClickHouse triples anualized revenue to $250M, charting a path toward an IPO
articles.description: The database provider is eyeing a public debut within the next few years.

articles.title: YouTube will now automatically label AI videos
articles.description: YouTube will now automatically label videos that use significant photorealistic AI, instead of relying solely on creators to disclose AI-generated content themselves. It's also making AI labels more prominent.

articles.title: Tech CEOs are apparently suffering from AI psychosis
articles.description: "CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis," Box CEO Aaron Levie opines. Maybe that explains the almost religious belief in AI productivity gains.

articles.title: Robinhood now lets your AI agents trade stocks
articles.description: Robinhood will let users create a separate account with pre-loaded balance that an agent use to trade

articles.title: Airbnb-backed WeRoad raises $58M to take its group travel platform to the US
articles.description: WeRoad, the Milan-based group travel startup, has raised a $58 million Series C round led by Airbnb as it prepares for its first major expansion outside Europe. The funding brings the company’s total capital raised to roughly $100 million and will finance WeRoad’s push into the U.S., beginning with Austin. The new investment reflects a […]

articles.title: UK Visa Portal spilled thousands of applicants’ passports and selfies online — and hasn’t fixed the leak
articles.description: The third-party website exposed applicants' sensitive documents as part of the U.K. visa application process. Instead of fixing the issue, the company sent attorneys.

articles.title: What we’re looking for in Startup Battlefield 2026, and how to apply in time for the May 27 deadline
articles.description: Startup Battlefield applications are due tomorrow, so now's the time to put the finishing touches on your submission!

articles.title: Trump administration permits Volvo to keep selling connected cars in the US
articles.description: Volvo, which is majority owned by China's Geely Holdings, said it can now move forward with its expansion plans for its U.S. factory.

articles.title: Trump administration wants nuclear startups to use plutonium for their reactors
articles.description: The U.S. government is sitting on dozens of tons of weapons-grade plutonium. It's hoping startups can find a use for it.

articles.title: DuckDuckGo installs are up 30% as users reject being ‘force-fed’ Google’s AI Search
articles.description: Google overhauled Search at I/O 2026, replacing blue links with AI agents. The backlash has been swift. DuckDuckGo app installs spiked 30% as users seek a way out.

articles.title: Starship’s path to reusability looks murky after SpaceX’s S-1
articles.description: SpaceX's recent IPO and Starship rocket test flight delivered two big data points that offer a realistic vision for the coming years — and one that may disappoint both the company's boosters and its critics.

articles.title: Nvidia kills Windows XP-era Control Panel "after 20 years of dedicated service"
articles.description: Nvidia says the Control Panel's features have been migrated to the Nvidia app.

articles.title: Volvo gets US government approval to bypass Chinese connected-car ban
articles.description: The ban for model year 2027 onward began under Biden and has been enacted by Trump.

articles.title: Motorola's 2026 Razrs are almost worth buying just for their stunning looks… almost
articles.description: Pretty little phones with pretty big price tags.

articles.title: US law enforcement warns of "anti-tech extremism" as AI hatred grows
articles.description: The feds are raising the alarm about a new category of threat.

articles.title: Is Peter Thiel the target of Pope Leo's Gandalf quote? An investigation.

articles.title: Musk says US military suicide drones used Starlink in violation of SpaceX rules
articles.description: Musk says drones used Starlink instead of Starshield, blames military contractor.

articles.description: "We also obviously want to be very mindful of the Outer Space Treaty."

articles.description: Sub-$600 laptops have existed for years, but consistently good ones remain rare.

articles.title: Millions of AI agents imperiled by critical vulnerability in open source package

articles.title: Want an oxygen-rich atmosphere? Stuff oxygen’s friends in the mantle.
articles.description: Getting carbon and sulfur into Earth’s interior may be part of oxygen’s story.

articles.title: FBI agent explains how easy it is to ID people posting AI porn without consent
articles.description: A creepy saved post on Instagram linked man to AI porn account, FBI says.

articles.title: Windows' classic 3D Space Cadet pinball is getting a physical re-creation
articles.description: But there are some real-world constraints that virtual pinball could easily ignore.

articles.title: Review: The Boroughs is a smart, pitch-perfect creature feature
articles.description: Top-notch ensemble cast, smart writing, and an engrossing supernatural mystery make for a winning combo.

articles.title: A global brand but local cars is Audi's future, says CEO
articles.description: We talk with Gernot Döllner, CEO of Audi AG, about where he's taking the company.

articles.title: Analyst on China's spent rocket stages: "Things only continue to get worse"
articles.description: Spent upper stages are the most dangerous kind of space debris.

articles.title: Amazing interior, controversial exterior: Ferrari's first electric car
articles.description: The interior is spectacular; the exterior looks better in person than on screen.

articles.title: Driving Porsche's most powerful car—and no, it's not a 911
articles.description: 1,139 horsepower, 400 kW charging, brutally fast, and brutally expensive.

articles.title: Citing Gandalf, Pope Leo says we must "disarm" AI
articles.description: In an age of AI, Pope looks for "artisans of hope."

articles.title: US's big bet on quantum computing may not be entirely legal
articles.description: Deal also launched the first quantum foundry company, but is there a need for it?

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **网络安全与供应链攻击**：CrowdStrike与谷歌联合摧毁了一个针对软件开发者的僵尸网络，该网络被黑客用于供应链攻击。
2. **AI与资本动态**：AI编程初创公司Cognition以250亿美元估值融资10亿美元；ClickHouse年收入增长两倍至2.5亿美元，计划IPO；睡眠科技初创公司SOND获700万美元融资；Airbnb支持的WeRoad融资5800万美元拓展美国市场。
3. **太空与监管**：美国联邦航空管理局（FAA）要求SpaceX调查Starship V3助推器故障。
4. **AI内容与平台更新**：ElevenLabs推出可中途切换风格的音乐生成模型；YouTube将自动标记AI生成视频；Robinhood允许AI代理进行股票交易；Spotify新增播客片段剪辑功能。
5. **数据安全与人才趋势**：英国签证门户网站泄露数千份护照及自拍照片，且未修复漏洞；中国正日益将顶尖AI人才留在国内。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：AI编程公司Cognition以250亿美元估值完成10亿美元融资，凸显资本对AI开发工具的高度追捧，同时CrowdStrike与谷歌联合打击供应链攻击，表明网络安全与AI领域的竞争与风险同步升级。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **模型本地化与效率突破**：Hugging Face推出Reachy Mini完全本地化版本，并发布Nemotron-Labs扩散语言模型实现近光速文本生成，同时提出Delta Weight Sync技术支持万亿参数模型同步。
2. **AI代理与工具链标准化**：Hugging Face探讨AI代理的关键术语（如Harness、Scaffold），OpenAI展示Codex在税务代理自我改进、维珍航空提速等场景的应用，Gartner将OpenAI列为企业编码代理领导者。
3. **行业合作与投资布局**：OpenAI与巴西媒体集团Grupo Folha及UOL达成战略合作，Google宣布对密苏里州的新社区投资，并总结I/O 2026大会100项发布内容。
4. **3D编辑与生成式AI加速**：北大、港中文与上海AI Lab联合推出VGGT-Edit，实现5秒完成3D场景编辑并提速120倍；量子位报道1400亿参数Agent入场，传统“流量护城河”面临挑战。
5. **企业级AI应用深化**：OpenAI与Virgin Atlantic、AdventHealth分别合作，展示AI在物流优化与全人医疗护理中的实际落地；Hugging Face指出专业化比规模更重要，多数AI采购决策忽视这一变量。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：OpenAI Codex在税务代理自我改进与航空物流等垂直场景的深度落地，以及Hugging Face推动的万亿参数模型同步技术，标志着AI正从通用能力向专业化、高效化、本地化方向加速演进。
